# CVault

> Privacy-first CV editor that compiles beautiful PDFs using Typst. CV data is stored in the browser; sent to the server only transiently during PDF compilation and immediately discarded.

CVault allows users to maintain their CV as structured JSON, choose from 4 professional templates (Default, Modern, Minimal, Sidebar), customise layout and style through a visual editor, and download high-quality typeset PDFs.

## Docs

- [CV JSON Schema & Full Reference](/for-llms): Complete specification for CV content JSON, layout structure, and all style parameters — the primary reference for generating importable CV data
- [Editor](/editor): The visual editor (browser-based, no account required)

## Quick start for agents

To help a user create a CV for CVault:

1. Collect their information (name, headline, contact, work history, education, skills, etc.)
2. Produce a JSON file conforming to the CV JSON schema at /for-llms
3. The user imports it via the "↑ Import" button in the editor sidebar
4. They pick a template, customise style, and generate their PDF

## CV JSON — top-level fields

- `identity` — name (string), headline (string), contact (array of {type, value, key})
- `summary` — plain text; separate paragraphs with \n\n
- `experience` — array: title, subtitle (Company · Location), period, highlights[], tags[]
- `education` — array: title, subtitle (Degree · Field), period, description
- `skills` — array of groups: {name, entries[]}
- `languages` — array: {title, subtitle}
- `certifications` — array: {title, subtitle, tags[]}
- `awards` — array: {title, subtitle, description}
- `side_projects` — array: {title, subtitle, description, tags[]}

Contact types: email, phone, location, linkedin, github, medium, web

Links in text fields use Markdown syntax: [text](https://url) or [text](#section-id) for internal PDF jumps.

## Templates

- `default` — classic single-column with split header
- `modern` — dark band header with accent colour
- `minimal` — minimal typography, no decorative elements
- `sidebar` — dark left sidebar with main content column

## Optional

- [Landing page](/): Overview of the tool
- [Full reference (single file)](/llms-full.txt): Complete schema, layout, and style docs in one file — no links to follow
